steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three destinations in a series, and each item contains an internal comma (a "where" clause). The question asks which punctuation option handles that correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — comma before 'and': keeps all separators as commas. When items in a series already contain commas, using commas to separate them creates ambiguity about where one item ends and the next begins. ✗
  • Choice B — semicolon before 'and to Moree': places semicolons between the three items, which is the standard rule for a "complex series" (items with internal punctuation). Produces: "drove to Wellington, where they interviewed locals; to Walgett, where they picketed a segregated cinema; and to Moree, where they staged a protest at a segregated public pool." ✓
  • Choice C — semicolon in wrong position: places the semicolon after "cinema" but before "and," breaking the parallel structure unevenly. ✗
  • Choice D — colon: a colon introduces a list or elaboration but is not used to separate items within a series. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
